It’s amazing how much a deep breath can do for your body
I feel tired of always trying to be the cleverest person in the room, and I want to let that desire go
Curiosity over cleverness, that is a more uplifting and noble value to uphold
Rather than learning new things to prove how great I am, I want to learn new things to find out how great the world can really be
There are so many curiosities out there to discover that I refuse to follow because I'm so caught up with trying to impress someone that might not really care after all
To my subconscious: there is no game to win anymore, so instead, let's try fucking around for a little bit and see what happens
Today, I stood up for my needs by asking my cousin for a hug, but I did not expect the gracious but forceful reaction from him not being able to give a “real hug”
It was at that moment, I understood how people can have extremely different tolerances and preferences on being vulnerable
Still reeling at the fact that I may have pushed people past their own limits of vulnerability, as I can be pretty soul-bearing when given the space to be
But, besides the point, I actually defended a need for my self instead of backing down from addressing it, great job past me!

yeah, i did a MURPH

M-y
U-pper body
R-eally
P-hucking
H-urts

it’s a workout where you

pull 100 ups
push 200 ups
squ 300 ats
run 3000 meters

since november 2020, i’ve been training to pull off a MURPH in one shot 

started out with doing a set of 10 pull ups, 20 push ups, 30 squats every day

took a lot of courage to keep showing up as the months flew by

it’s beginning to sink in how much my body and my mind has evolved over the past year

i started off this journey insecure about my body image and personal willpower

now i can make a stupid meme about it without a sweat

survive. don’t die. don’t quit.

earnestly speaking about your experience is the best way to learn how to stand up for your self
the more I spend time on here, I feel my courage deficit run even shorter when attempting to talk about parts of my self that have felt injustice

growing up, I carried a gnawing feeling that I was not a person worth standing up for. although I had friends and family that came to my rescue, I could feel my sovereignty slowly slip away from me as I relied on their arguments for my redeemability

my heart goes out for the little guy, poor and confused, never believing that he could truly speak for his salvation. he knew exactly what it looked like, but he was too afraid to stumble towards success
Now I am speaking on his behalf, truly claiming that salvation is possible. We can shape our reality, question everything, speak the fuck up because no one else has the power that we have TOGETHER

making 100 beats sounds hard. making 100 loops that take inspiration from my favorite producers' techniques and styles sounds a little bit easier.

who are the producers that i deeply resonate with and how feasible is it to emulate specific pieces of their production vibe?

making 100 beats in a month sounds impossible. workable time constraints can make it possible.

what’s a feasible timetable that I can implement throughout the month that would allow making 100 beats expedient and sustainable?

making 100 beats sounds expensive. delving into environment design seems important.

what’s the minimum amount of equipment that i need to make these beats without limiting my potential creative palette?

making 100 beats sounds normal. live on twitch, it sounds innovative.

what twitch native features can i leverage to augment my production process?

making 100 beats sounds boring. performing 100 beats sounds like a show.

how can i craft my production process to appeal towards the audience that i want?

the timeline in the wee early morning has some potent energy, a quiet lucidity

in this state, i feel compelled to take stock of my time here on the birdsite’s tucked-in wing

it’s wonderful, albeit intimidating

in trying to speak true of my experience, a part of me always felt performative about the whole practice

grew up as a natural bullshitter that was pleased to play my roles well; as an obedient child, student, friend

so upon meeting ppl here, i felt y’all deserved better

as i grew fonder toward my new friends, a lot of pressure was put on myself to improve FOR THEM

but upon introspection, the gap between how I presented myself and how i perceived myself grew vaster to the point where i was afraid to say anything at all

would u like me like dis?

there are days when I don’t feel coherent with my ideal but still wanna share whatever’s in my head, and the facade that I have enforced upon myself to follow keeps me flinched up

fuck likes, fuck qts, fuck follower ratios; I just wanna make friends and show em dope shit, bro

a friend dm’d me saying that the sun will rise and you will be freer

now finding it cosmically hilarious that it took me waking up before the sun and finding the space to breathe, away from everyone’s potential attention to tap into a sense of freedom

this is for you kiddo. the one who was paranoid over prying eyes, feeling too vulnerable to act differently than who you think you are

this is our space now. and it’s bigger than you know. 💞

this surfaced a familiar frustration that i had about an empty textarea, asking me "What's happening?"

but i just recently noticed how it echoed deep seated frustrations of the empty page while i was in school

so, like any reasonable FAN, i vented this frustration in a whirlwind stream-of-consciousness journal entry, which i've lovingly entitled as a [[SQUIGGLE SCREECH]]:

a little game where i write 1000 words in 25 minutes

i've grown familiar to the grind of running past my self-censors in the safety of my roam graph, but it always caught up at the place where i __really__ wanted to start playing in

and as the word count started to grow in my peripheral vision, i wrote: "let's play a new game"

sure, these story beats could be compressed to one tweet or maybe i could tighten up some unnecessary details, but poaster brain only care about number go up

so i'm willing to put my bets on the number that really matters

finally finished my first read-through of @visakanv's introspect

i think it's safe to say that this book has gently and casually shattered my self-concept

@visakanv the book really advocates one major move: take quick notes

but the real groundbreaking stuff happens when you READ and INVESTIGATE the notes you leave behind

i've been anxious to read the [[SQUIGGLE SCREECHES]] that i've left behind for three months, but reading how gentle visa was in personal journey of introspection through the book's "ramblings" gave me the courage to delve into my own writing

it was cool to recognize parts of my own writing that i could personally improve: personal voice, word choice, pacing

reading thru my entries, i felt like a teacher grading my own essays, but it felt oddly nourishing. i then asked myself, "why wasn't writing fun LIKE THIS?"

my mind flashed back to all of the times when i wrote while being educated: stressed, perfectionistic, constrained

and then it hit me. i loved writing. i always have. just never on other people's terms.

i felt this wave of sadness for all of the times i had put my internal voice of articulation under so much scrutiny and doubt

my fear to read my writing was a fear of subjecting myself to the tyrant that i was secretly so familiar with

but no longer.

growing up, i quietly held tight onto a dream to become a musician as it kept me company through the hoops of prestige and meritocracy

after reading through my notes alongside visa's threads, that dream grew hollow and broken

but a curious light peeked through.

a memory bubbled through the surface: me at two years old, reading to a group of curious toddlers.

my first relationship was with music, but my first love was with the written word.

i wrote in my latest squiggle screech: "all that we need to be okay is to write."

thank you so much @visakanv for allowing your passion for words to shine through your book, as it reignited mine.

@KasraKaley haha yea, an anecdote from my mom incepted this memory deep into my body-palace

i didn’t believe her at first, but reading through my past made me viscerally remember it again

you're allowed to have bottle-necked courage
there's a special joy that comes with saying something and not knowing where you'd end up
been experimenting with this through a status game that i've been solo developing, the [[SQUIGGLE SCREECH]]
if you're curious to see what that's been like, you can check out a demonstration of it on muh substack: https://t.co/FFaDtz1PGl
for the past three months, i've been slowly grinding out thousands upon thousands of words, slicing through the weeds of my subconscious. perfecting my form, feeling the weight of my squiggles
all of this has been constant practice and preparation for bringing out my form in the public commons, one courageous utterance at a time
one thing that i noticed from cultivating this practice is how my muscles atrophy? tense up? when i attempt to swing out on the timeline
tempted to chalk my tweeting bottleneck to performance anxiety, but after my grinding, i'm beginning to see that the flinch comes from something other than just being misunderstood

remembering all the moments when plaintext had brought feelings of unexpected embarrassment, pain, sorrow

the bottleneck acts as a protective mechanism
before i stumbled upon the teapot, it felt like a fresh kind of hell to publicly share all your thoughts out into the void
looking back, it only seemed that way because i wasn't open to being surprised by the love and curiosity that can come back from it https://t.co/0hnUt1VD9F

we can choose to remember and experience this juicy surprise at every moment

all that needs to be done is to slowly unfurl
don't worry guys, they were just playing a nice game of fronthand backhand
the waft of drizzled grass

whoops, accidentally been equating my inherent value with how much money i can earn per month

sooooo. for the past few months, i've felt a lot of shame around articulating a deep need for agency around my personal sense of safety and security, which has been manifesting thru job/apartment hunting, but starting to see that it's also motivated by a deep feeling of mistrust

feels real tough to admit this, but there's a lack of self-trust in being able to provide for myself beyond what others can define as "employable", a lack of trust in my friends that they can also see the value of what i do for a job
or rather, that my friends value me beyond however i decide to earn my living

a part of me feels like i owe it to my past self to grind for  "the coveted six-figure income" after suffering through a grueling engineering undergrad, only to be birthed into adulthood during [gestures at everything]

but now, it's starting to become clearer that this mfer was always in survival mode, unable to be open to the idea that you don't have to love the thing you do to make money, and that life happens outside of work
the world opens up when you do

this feels embarrassing to admit but running really helps me understand the myth of sisyphus
coming off the joggers high, the felt-tip knowledge is starting to slip; but i find it funny that i’m deciding to tweet about it

making a thread is like pushing a boulder up a hill: blissfully pointless. feeling the inertia on my skin freezes me. why exert effort when you can sit still and see other people climb up the mountaintop?

but then, the ground begins to move under you and the rock rolls upward. shit, did i do that?

oop it rolls back down
then you try again with the knowledge of your past efforts behind you, a foundation to step on
soon enough, you gain momentum and confidence as the load becomes lighter to bear and all you need to do is to just keep moving
hallucinations come, as all past efforts rush through you and live out their silent tensions with each step. keep going keep going keep going
it hits you, maybe that’s why people keep running up that hill: kinship, the rush, whatever the fuck you wanna call it

you feel sad for all the past times sitting on top of that boulder to watch others fall and shield yourself with their projected pain, as pushing this goddamn thing up wasn’t as painful as just sitting there

then you feel the pangs of jealousy that others make boulder-rolling look easy, and then feel safe in the false confidence that staying on the ground is much safer than trudging through the hell of constantly going up and down

then you realise you’re almost at the top, so you might as well finish this up

but fuck when the hell did this get so heavy
can i just stop here? ugh staying near the top is more tiring than actually getting there,,, you’re slowing down,,, fuck it explooooode

gogogogogogogogogogogogogogogogogogogogogogogogogogogogogogogogogogogogogogogogogogogogogogogogogogogogogogogogogogogogogogogogogogogogogogogogogogogogogogogogogogogogogogogogogogogogogogogogogogogogogogogogogogogogogogogogogogogogogogogogogogogogogogogogogogogogogogogogogogogogo
and stop.

you sit there, on the top, looking out and watch everything whizz by. distracted from all the activity, you decide to look in. 

you say thank you, and i’m sorry.

suddenly the boulder shifts and tilts as it can’t stay there for long, so you decide to let it go.

not before you decide to kick the thing back down

you sprint back down to meet its pace, gravity your ally in bringing you and your boulder back down to earth

and you could swear that for a split second, your feet floats with your gait as if you’re flying

but nah, that’s just the earth flying towards YOU

feet and face planted firmly on the floor, you shake it off and say

“lmao, can we try again?”

oooh oomf reading this is bringing up a lot of cringe in my chest, that’s how you know i really needed to say this
okay breathing through this and situating within the gut, feels right to bring up some context
talking with some friends helped me clarify a deep fear: being perceived as a person who is “unreliable”; quite literally ungrounded
i’ve hidden myself from this fear by leaning into being unavailable as a part of my personality: constantly moving around, taking days to respond to anything
and doing all of those things are fine, UNTIL it started disservicing a deep part of me that desired stability and security
i gave up my romantic vagabard role to service myself as a normie office worker back home and i’ve never felt more at peace
it’s a peace that i’ve been looking to speak on behalf of, to prepare for and hand off to the russell that’s more confident and courageous,,, until i found that desire to speak contentious
that tension comes from being insecure about whether this peace that i’m speaking from is legitimate: can i trust myself to speak that i’m ‘’finally’’ ‘’healed’’?
and to that i say “FUCK OFF, you’re in the way of telling my friends that i’m okay”
focusing on whether im okay or not okay to speak has prevented me from actually speaking up,,, LMAO WHAT THE ACTUAL FUCK

whoa turns out you can compassionately gaze into your eyes in the mirror and watch yourself feel better

if you cradle the eyes long enough, it feels less like "watching" and more like "witnessing", as if the contours of the face subtly shift and weave beyond one's ability to control it. it just happens.
i've done this a couple of times when feeling things out was too much to bear alone, but it's surprising how consistent it can be as a method to really face myself with care and concern

contrasting this with my writing process, i'm now seeing how easy it can be to hide how i'm feeling behind my words, and even easier it can be to hide behind those feelings as a way of expressing what i want
on the glass, you can experience it in all its messy humanness. a language before the word, achingly familiar yet devestatingly foreign
on the glass i can see how i'm not as despicable as i think when i point my eyes to the world, on the glass when i reflect the world back onto myself
on the glass, i remember. i want to connect. i want to understand.

so yea i just quit my dayjob https://t.co/2aj6aqMgkk
i was attempting to draft something up in my head to poast into circles about this decision but nothing felt right so imma just write through it
been agonizing over it for days, attempting to find the right framing, have a good clarifying statement for what message i wanted to convey but i’m already getting the ick just taking about it
what feels alive right now is tied in with faith, not knowing how inconceivably wrong i am but still going into it anyways
the decision to leave was comically simple, felt like a rpg dialogue screen where a pivotal moment telegraphs the fact that you’re going on an adventure
took a pause to recollect all the choices i’ve made before and how different the intention is to head back out into uncertainty once again
i worked hard at the job in the hopes that i can build confidence as a programmer, pay off my student loans, and spend quality time at home with family and friends
coming out of it now, i can say that i have gained a deeper comfort in my faculties and limits in programming, as well as demystifying the damoclean debt that hung over my head
but really, i’ve been feeling really isolated as a result of going so deep into proving myself as a competent worker
it’s the thinly veiled existential question of “what value do i provide to people?” that i’ve doggedly framed as “what am i worth in the marketplace?”
reading that may seem comical or trivial, but thar be load bearing questions that underpin a majority of my drives as a person

i want to be valuable
i want to be excellent
i want to make good shit
i want to elevate the fucken game
i want to leave it better than i found it

but it’s also just what i want
i’ve lost touch with being in service to other people, checking in with folks, sharing the goodness around me

(okay wow not tweeting in public for months and seven different threads come out all at once)

to loop back into what i wanted to talk about at the beginning of all this, leaving the dayjob was a recommitment toward having faith in myself again

i’ve gotten what i’ve gotten out of it, and am so much stronger because of it

here i am, showing the fuck up

yea that feels like a good place to stop.

gn y’all, see you tomorrow

the most practical conceptual handle i’ve encountered that bears little to no mention around these conversations is the divide between literacy and orality https://t.co/8rfCSftHlD

orality, as i understand it, is a culture of communication mediated by speech

or y’know, talking to people

the reason why i find it so useful is that the limits of language are baked into its discourse

particularly, the literature around it has charted a history of how we started by keeping stories within tribes through spoken word, only to be transformed by the invention of writing

as for how useful orality is as a model for solving problems:

just think about the times when you were having trouble arranging the right words in an essay or a tweet

and then you stumble into the right phrase by talking through what you want to say with a friend https://t.co/5SvumZcJc5

there’s honestly a lot of work to be done here, and i’m just getting started

but for anyone who thinks that i’m saying that writing is bad and reading is a curse and retvrn to oral culture, i’ll leave you with a quote from Walter J. Ong https://t.co/Cz5iUc7sP2

looking at project xanadu's system of bidirectional linking as a "vaporware project" and can't help but feel that it hasn't gone off the ground due to insufficient levels of impermanence https://t.co/PKXLl8Jqud

the utility of hyperlinked text comes in the shared context of words across different documents, but as these documents become overwrought with sources, attention gets redirected away from the words being in play

for instance, some words come across better when said by different people. inspiration for those words may not originate by their interlocutor, but it doesn't serve the spirit of the word if we fixate on attributing to its source as it distracts from what wants to be communicated

forgetting aids remembering, so any system that wants to incorporate stronger resonances in meaning making has to solve for ephemerality in the right places. leave clues for those who want to find out, but make sure you keep your audience entranced and engaged https://t.co/MiZSHfUGhX

feeling through the differences between writing and speaking, and some revealed preferences are coming up https://t.co/1b4l5WuSsa

i’d much rather talk to people than tweet. even writing down this sentence brings up a lot of revulsion, mainly because i’d have to imagine something to respond with rather than flowing with what feels immediately present in conversation with someone
the inputs especially feel different, how everything gets head-filtered through an imagined audience, which can be quite overbearing to keep up

huh, it’s pretty overbearing due to how negative it can end up being. write enough and you end up contending with your worst fears and neuroses and it’s harder to escape it when you have nothing but your mind to respond to
but is it worth it to escape? are they not also equally deserving of a response? what would happen if you look and them in the eye and talk to them as if you’re speaking to anyone else?
there’s a lingering assumption that buzzes in my body as if it’s going into shock. but my heartbeat is also there, ready to return to when i give myself the chance to listen to it
i’d like to keep friends around who call out your neuroses and hold you in kindness to feel uncomfortable enough with it and let the feeling pass

some patterns of thought that felt spiraling or constrictive land as flinches in the body— configurations of tensions that made my body feel achy to be in
it’s actually useful to switch between the two modes, as asking the right question can quite literally give your body the space to relax and ease itself

so i recently decided to lock my account as an exercise in locality. feel like i've grown enough personal relationships with people from twitter that i want to be broadcasting explicitly for those who choose to tune into what i want to say
it feels cosier— i have the space to contextualise and elaborate without second-guessing the scrutiny of a wider audience
although i have locked this account in the past, this time it feels different because there is so much more that i'm ready and willing to share

this decision also comes with the acceptance that i don't need to grow in followers because the people who are already here are valuable to me and to each other

attention metrics be unflattening here

ehhh okay feel like elaborating on the "growing in followers" bit, but i'm basically focusing on quality over quantity of attention and locking feels like it pushes me to consider who i'm writing for more deeply

if you’re looking for a more accessible way to explore somatics and embodiment, Latent Insight is a simple enough practice that gets at the heart of what can come up when you start listening to your body https://t.co/LEAzH8CaCf

for the sake of demonstration, let's do some live-tweeting on what my body wants to say around this notion of privacy that i've been experimenting with for the past couple of months https://t.co/GGYPjDzuqg

funny enough, there's not a lot that my body wants to really say at the moment,, this is where some training in the LI practice can be of good use: by prompting different questions that allows the body to sense itself

my fav one is: "do you need to use the bathroom?"

no need to have the question answered with a response or an action, the important thing is to have the sense of the question open up or change your experience of the body that can make the listening more available to you

this is where practicing with other people in a container can be useful, these subtle movements that feel a bit silly or benign to tune into can actually feel more safe to speak when in the presence of folks who treat their somatic signals with care and skill

today i'm daydreaming about the backpacks i'd carry around with me while going to school, fabric stretching more than necessary, zippers barely bursting at the seams. my parents would see this and take it off my shoulders, but even they would remark "mai gahd why dis so hebby"

and they're valid, there's no good reason why it is. but i liked the weight, and some part of me thought of packing for any occasion cuz "you never know what could happen". never really registered to kid-me how much my back hurt or my shoulders getting sore, worth the struggle

there was a comfort to being able to live out of my backpack, starting young by flying around on standby around the asia-pacific trained me for it. my family had a sense of mobility that felt normal, like "yea sure we can visit dad in yokosuka, but finish your homework"

am truly blessed to Know that the world is big from such an early age, but that came at a cost of not knowing what home can really feel like that my friends growing up got to enjoy. my beautiful island territory was just the pond i swam in until i can wade through oceans

and so i carried my backpack with me, wherever i went. the only thing that kept me grounded down to earth, to remember, to be prepared, to get where i need to go. i'd soon find my current out, but outside these easeful tides, i was unprepared for the thrashing that came my way

getting wiped out is disorienting, where you can't help but wonder "how did that get me?" even though the waves come for us all. so i did what i knew best and kept stuffing my backpack, the pressure wrapping me like a safety blanket, feet only taking so much floor until collaps-

...slowed down to a crawl in open ocean, the burden behind my back felt like the only home i had left. just keep swimming, just keep swimming, until you find somewhere to land and take a rest. soon enough, u see more fish needing rest, u team up & move around. maybe that's home?

we help each other, and these fish eventually change and grow into creatures beautiful and majestic, while i still got this damn shell on my back. we get carried away into different currents, so i remain swimming along. pass by more sealife, more exotic but still the same water

the longer i swim, the greater i appreciate folks strokes along the sea. mimic their movements, their grace, yet they move along their way, me not too far behind. i get a better feel of my own glide, and i notice that glide in other folks style. oh my gosh i'm helping too!

> be me
> turn twenty-seven
> recall the past 365 days
> months after quitting my day job on my beautiful island territory
> start reading about language, media, communication, connect some dots
> friend invites me to continue research at month-long pop up campus in PORTO
> pack my suitcase, say farewell to family and friends
> “oh this is for the long haul ain’t it”
> involve myself within the microwave of interpersonal dynamics
> surface and integrate deep-seated memories around abuse and manipulation
> can’t go back now, find my path leading to Tirana and decompress
> oh gosh now i’m really homeless
> land with running habit, 5k almost every day
> feet lead me to massachusetts, reconnect with college friends and new acquaintances
> trip to the woods, connect with some animal spirits
> “tame the concrete jungle”
> start alpha testing life in NYC 
> euro friends nudge me onto a week long side quest in berlin amongst the trees
> spend time with soul fam, deep healing and love and magic
> return to bushwick, enmeshing into community
> all the moving makes me insecure and shut down
> “fuck i need to get serious about my life”
> open up and humble myself enough to ask for help
> things start to smoothen out, start applying for myself, things looking up
> get a missed call from my sister
> “she’s in the hospital”
> last living elder passes, drop everything for my grandma
> pick back up my tender heart
> start dialing in at a programming retreat in downtown brooklyn
> impostor syndrome heightens, start to notice how much it’s self-inflicted
> haunted by past, start seeing through the walls of how much i get trapped into proving myself
> in school, in community, around parts of twitter
> lean into gentleness and humility, start letting go of hiding behind illegibility
> communicate more honestly with the people around me
> start opening up, pushing more out of the way
> life is becoming less of a mess, more comfortable handling my own messes

> i can see more of the mess around me
> huh okay
> be stupid enough to try and handle it anyways

literature aside, this meme is probably the most realistic and compassionate depiction i've encountered that gestures at the problem of measuring and comparing IQ https://t.co/EejN5bVvpk https://t.co/7po7yHQAVS
the tumblr comments that are normally attached to the meme also adds some heft and context, if only ogre can see that other folks are rooting for his intellectual journey https://t.co/wkOVBkYifv

the problem that ogre experiencing is how most people normally flatten IQ as implicitly measuring how "smart" or "stupid" someone is, when the two labels aren't even supposed to be conflated to begin with https://t.co/aePQfxtonX

if you start treating these two measures as separate, you start to realize that stupidity is courageous, and following what your heart wants to know means feeling your feelings and looking snot-nosed and helpless, which not many people have the,, courage for

and frankly, the tragedy of ogre comes from his inability to see that his strength to face what he doesn't know is **the** guiding force for understanding anything at all. hopefully he has an ogre book club with other ogre friends to appreciate how strong he really is

but if ogre going through this alone, hope he also gets to see how it's okay to be teary-eyed and brave about the things he cares to know, rather than dead-eyed and stoic about what's not worth knowing in the first place https://t.co/jl1MQviGgJ

as an aside: it’d probably be to the benefit of dumb students who want to develop their writing skills to replace the Five Paragraph Essay with the Five Tweet Thread https://t.co/rxT88LUWUF

for one, it’s economically fit to the value and scarcity of attention in the modern day. 

people are too numb to gaze over double spaced times new roman to seek information, but they’d look into the replies if they actually want to know more about what the first tweet offers

second, paragraphs are way too expansive of a container for students to one-shot their knowledge of a given topic. 

meet students where they’re at with the constraints they’re more familiar with and they’ll be appropriately challenged to engage in writing with clarity of intent

finally, it’s actually more enjoyable to write tweet threads than paragraphs, especially for beginners.

as a person who’s struggled the tedium and grief of writing poorly, threading as a medium expanded the possibilities of what i thought “expression through words” was or can be

anyways i’m just warming up for @threadapalooza so if you’re interested in getting better at thinking clearly through writing while enjoying the process, consider threading

read what other people are thinking about, hang out in the replies, quote tweet thoughts that engage you https://t.co/qjXfaX03Zz
